items.name: Senolytics coverage repair assessment
items.summary: The senolytics track now has an auditable vertical slice across preclinical D+Q discovery/healthspan evidence, early human biomarker and feasibility studies, a phase 2 randomized bone trial, a 2025 cognition/mobility pilot, translation-review context, risk-of-bias records, and registry-only active trial-watch records. It is adequate as a maintainable knowledge-base slice, but not yet ready for quantitative synthesis.

evidence_categories.rationale: Includes early D+Q human biomarker, randomized IPF feasibility, phase 2 bone, and AD-risk cognition/mobility pilot records.
Includes foundational discovery work plus the old-mouse healthspan and post-treatment survival paper.
Cellular senescence targeting is anchored by the discovery source and translation review, but pathway-level extraction is not yet complete.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future extraction should separate senescent-cell survival pathways, SASP markers, and tissue-specific clearance evidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The IPF, bone, and AD-risk records explicitly encode mixed, pilot, primary/subgroup, and feasibility limitations instead of flattening them into positive claims.
Safety-oriented records exist, but adverse events, dosing cadence, durability, and discontinuation details still need explicit extraction.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Add adverse-event tables and dosing-duration fields before safety synthesis.

evidence_categories.rationale: Two active no-results trial-watch records were added, but the full senolytics registry surface still needs a broader surveillance pass.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Add deferred fisetin frailty registries and associated no-results status records in the next surveillance pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current slice includes a narrative translation review but no systematic-review or meta-analysis record.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Search specifically for recent systematic reviews, trial reviews, and evidence maps.

evidence_categories.rationale: Records separate senolytic mechanism/intervention class from evidence tier, clinical indication, and registry status, reducing the risk that the track becomes an unsupported efficacy bucket.

known_gaps.description: The D+Q bone RCT now has registry-extracted endpoint-level outcome/result records, but DKD, IPF, and AD-risk human studies remain triage-level; the bone RCT also still needs full publication table and supplement reconciliation.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run extraction-refresh passes for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk, then reconcile the bone RCT registry extraction against full article tables and supplements.

known_gaps.description: Aggregate adverse-event counts were extracted for the D+Q bone RCT, but event-specific adverse-event terms, discontinuation details, dosing cadence, and durability details remain incomplete across the human evidence slice.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Add event-specific safety outcomes/results or a dedicated adverse-event schema, then extend safety extraction across DKD, IPF, and AD-risk studies.

known_gaps.description: Only two active no-results registry records were added in this repair pass; broader fisetin and D+Q trial surveillance remains incomplete.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a surveillance pass over ClinicalTrials.gov for senolytics, D+Q, fisetin, and senescence-targeting terms.

known_gaps.description: No recent systematic-review or meta-analysis source has been screened into the knowledge base.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Search for recent systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and trial-landscape reviews.

items.next_coverage_action: Run extraction_refresh on the human D+Q result-bearing studies, prioritizing exact outcome definitions, quantitative effects, adverse events, and prespecified versus exploratory analysis labels.
consumer_warnings: Coverage is not synthesis-ready; use as a scoped evidence map or work queue, not as a pooled estimate basis.
High-priority gaps remain; downstream claims should preserve the known-gap context.

items.name: Senolytics Scaffold Coverage Assessment
items.summary: This assessment only proves the new repository scaffold can represent a source-backed senolytics evidence slice. It is not a full coverage assessment for the track.

evidence_categories.rationale: One foundational senolytic discovery source has been represented, but the broader seminal anchor set is not yet audited.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Run a real coverage-repair pass before treating the senolytics work scope as source-complete.

evidence_categories.rationale: No human senolytic trials have been ported into this scaffold yet.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Human D+Q, fisetin, and adjacent direct-senolytic trial records should be screened in the first real bootstrap or repair pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: The stricter track taxonomy now records inclusion, exclusion, and boundary notes for senolytics, but neighboring tracks are not yet represented.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Add neighboring senomorphic and immune-clearance boundaries before broader classification work.

known_gaps.description: The scaffold contains one preclinical discovery source but no direct human senolytics evidence.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a bounded senolytics coverage-repair pass to add human trial, registry, safety, and limiting-evidence records.

known_gaps.description: The scaffold has not yet implemented result extraction or synthesis compatibility rules.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Add result and synthesis schemas before attempting evidence pooling.

items.next_coverage_action: Use this scaffold to drive the first real senolytics coverage-repair pass.
consumer_warnings: Coverage is not synthesis-ready; use as a scoped evidence map or work queue, not as a pooled estimate basis.
High-priority gaps remain; downstream claims should preserve the known-gap context.
Superseded by senolytics-coverage-repair-2026-06-21 for this track/hallmark scope.
